title.scholar: Alexander Graham Bell Invents the Telephone

summary.scholar: On March 10, 1876, Alexander Graham Bell made the first successful telephone call, saying to his assistant Thomas Watson, 'Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.' Bell had patented his invention just days earlier. The telephone would revolutionize communication, eventually connecting millions of Americans and transforming business, social life, and government.
summary.explorer: On March 10, 1876, Alexander Graham Bell made the very first phone call! He said to his helper: 'Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.' It doesn't sound very exciting, but this invention would change the ENTIRE world. Imagine never being able to call anyone!

significance.scholar: The telephone transformed human communication more fundamentally than any invention since the printing press, creating new industries and reshaping society.
significance.explorer: Before the telephone, the only way to talk to someone far away was to write a letter or send a telegraph. Bell's invention let people actually HEAR each other's voices!

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
